mission.title: Install and Configure Keylime on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keylime is an open-source scalable trust system that leverages TPM technology to bootstrap and maintain trust on edge, cloud, and IoT devices. Installing Keylime allows you to ensure the integrity of remote machines and securely manage cryptographic trust.

steps.title: Add the Keylime Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Keylime Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add keylime https://keylime.dev/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Keylime using Helm
steps.description:
Install Keylime with the specified version and create a namespace for it:
```bash
helm install keylime keylime/keylime --version 7.14.1 --namespace keylime --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Keylime components are running correctly:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace keylime
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Keylime components by editing the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade keylime keylime/keylime --namespace keylime --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Keylime
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow Keylime to access necessary resources:
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: keylime
  name: keylime-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: keylime-role-binding
  namespace: keylime
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: keylime-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: keylime
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Keylime will show all Keylime pods running in the 'keylime' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for secure operation.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add keylime https://keylime.dev/charts
helm repo update
helm upgrade keylime keylime/keylime --namespace keylime --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: keylime
  name: keylime-role
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
EOF

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: keylime-role-binding
  namespace: keylime
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: keylime-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: keylime
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Keylime Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Keylime.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the Keylime namespace and verify that all resources have been removed.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Keylime installation.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to install the latest version of Keylime.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check that the Keylime components are running correctly after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue may occur if there is a misconfiguration or missing dependencies. Use the following command to check the logs of the pod: 
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace keylime
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during upgrade, check the installed version and the available versions with: 
```bash
helm list --namespace keylime
helm search repo keylime --versions
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions errors
troubleshooting.description:
If Keylime is unable to access resources, verify the Role and RoleBinding configurations. Check the current roles with: 
```bash
kubectl get roles --namespace keylime
kubectl get rolebindings --namespace keylime
```

troubleshooting.title: Service unavailable errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see service unavailable errors, ensure that all Keylime components are running. Check the status of the pods with: 
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace keylime
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
